name: Fan Jian (Changyuan)
page_title: Fan Jian (Changyuan) 樊建 (長元) [Shu, Wei, Jin] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: Prefect of the Masters of Writing
historical_notes:
Fan Jian, styled Changyuan, was from Yiyang commandery.

He served as a colonel and was sent to Wu as an emissary in 251. Sun Quan was very ill at the time, so they were unable to meet. Still, Sun Quan asked Zhuge Ke, “How does Fan Jian compare to Zong Yu?” Zhuge Ke replied, “His talent and knowledge do not compare to [Zong] Yu, but his noble nature surpasses him.”

He later served as Palace Attendant and Director of the Central Depository (中書令) before in late 258, replacing Dong Jue as Director of the Secretariat. Fan Jian, along with Dong Jue and Zhuge Zhan, unsuccessfully tried to curb the increasing political influence of Huang Hao and the many military campaigns Jiang Wei launched against Wei. Nevertheless, Fan Jian would never fully associate himself with Huang Hao, rejecting any offers from the latter.

After Shu fell in 263, Fan Jian was sent to Luoyang in the spring of 264, joining the army of Sima Zhao. He was also enfeoffed as a marquis. In autumn, he was sent back to Shu as Regular Mounted Attendant to soothe the lands of Shu, as he was a familiar figure. He is mentioned in the Table of Jin Officials, showing that he served Jin. Once, Jin emperor Sima Yan asked Fan Jian about how Zhuge Liang governed of Shu. Fan Jian responded, “When he heard evil, he would always correct it, and he did not boast excessively. The faith in his rewards and punishments is enough to impress the deities.” Sima Yan replied, “Excellent! If I got such a person as to assist me, how can there be any difficulties today?” However, Fan Jian bowed his head to the ground and said, “I listened to the discussions of the world, and all of them say that Deng Ai was treated wrongly. Your Majesty knows this, but has not fixed it. Is this perhaps what Feng Tang would say of this: ‘Getting [Lian] Po and [Li] Mu but being unable to use them.'?” Sima Yan laughed and said, “I was about to ask as to understand this, yet your words have outpaced my thoughts!” After the conversation, Sima Yan released an edict rehabilitating Deng Ai posthumously.

full_text:
Fan Jian (Changyuan) 樊建 (長元)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Fan Jian (樊建), styledChangyuan (長元), was born in Yiyang. Did not associate with Huang Hao. Went with Liu Shan when Shu fell. Fan Jian was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom, the Wei Kingdom, and the Jin Dynasty. Fan Jian lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
